picks.narrative: The model sees KC as a 51.6% side; the market is pricing 43.2%, leaving a 8.7pp edge. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The main pushback is home field (~-0.7pp): MET's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +133 still sits inside the playable range (to +110). This is mostly a price play — the model likes the number more than the matchup, so the baseball case is mixed.

picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on CHC by 7.5pp — 52.9% fair against a 44.8% price. No individual baseball factor clears the bar for a headline here — this is not a single visible mismatch, it is the model pricing the side differently than the market. The main pushback is pitcher quality (xfip) (~-0.6pp): David Peterson 4.77 xFIP vs Trevor Rogers 4.13 xFIP. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +120 still sits inside the playable range (to +104). The read here is the price, not the profile — the model just makes this a better side than the posted number does.

picks.narrative: A 5.2pp gap: IBP has MIA at 48.3%, the market at 43.7%. The largest lift is defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp): The model's defensive-efficiency adjustment favors the pick. The main pushback is pitcher quality (xfip) (~-2.0pp): Janson Junk 4.69 xFIP vs Bryce Miller 2.55 xFIP. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +132 still sits inside the playable range (to +126). The edge is the model pricing MIA differently than the market rather than one of the marquee starter/lineup/bullpen buckets — worth taking at the number, not chasing past it.

picks.narrative: IBP values MIN 5.1pp higher than the market — 51.0% model vs 45.6% implied. The largest lift is lineup quality (~+0.7pp): MIN projects above its season baseline against this pitcher handedness. The main pushback is pitcher quality (xfip) (~-2.2pp): Bailey Ober 5.93 xFIP vs Gavin Williams 3.57 xFIP. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +118 still sits inside the playable range (to +113). This is value in the number more than the matchup — the model just makes MIN a better side than the price does.

picks.narrative: IBP makes OAK 49.4% fair versus a 45.2% no-vig market price — a 4.3pp gap. There's no standout starter, lineup, or bullpen signal driving this — the gap comes from the model's baseline read of the side, not a visible matchup edge. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +122 still sits inside the playable range (to +120). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.
